metadata.author: David Ancor
metadata.stakeholders: Founders; Product Managers; Engineering Teams; Team Admins; Compliance Officers; Site Reliability / DevOps
metadata.parent_okr: Ship c1v v1.0 with metric-traceable specs and live feedback loop
metadata.regulatory_refs: SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; PCI-DSS (scope tbd in Module 4)

initial_conditions: A spec exists in 'draft' status in the Product Manager's organization.
The Product Manager is authenticated (cross-cutting UC14).
The Product Manager holds review permission on the spec's organization.
The spec has passed UC01 validation (schema + metric traceback coverage).

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Product Manager navigates to the spec review queue

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall authenticate the Product Manager's session before exposing the review queue (CC.R01).
The system shall enumerate every spec in 'draft' status that is assigned to the authenticated Product Manager's review queue.
The system shall return the spec list sorted by submission time ascending and rendered within REVIEW_QUEUE_LOAD_BUDGET_MS.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Product Manager selects a spec from the review queue

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall verify the Product Manager holds review permission on the selected spec's organization.
The system shall load the spec bundle including the spec document, diagrams, and constants table at the spec's committed hash.
The system shall render the loaded spec bundle in a reviewable view within SPEC_RENDER_BUDGET_MS.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Product Manager reads the rendered spec and drafts review comments
Product Manager submits each review comment anchored to a spec section

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall persist each submitted review comment with Product Manager identity, spec section anchor, and timestamp.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Product Manager selects a review action from {approve, reject, request_revisions}

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall validate that every 'request_revisions' action includes at least one review comment.
The system shall transition the spec state from 'draft' to the state implied by the review action within STATE_TRANSITION_BUDGET_MS.
The system shall record an audit entry for the state transition with reviewer identity, prior state, new state, comment count, and timestamp (CC.R02).

actor_steps_table.other_actors: Founder receives notification of the review decision and comments.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall notify the spec's Founder of the review decision and attached comments.
The system shall enable the CLI-emission pipeline (<<extend>> UC04) on approve and block it on reject or request_revisions.

actor_steps_table.extra_actor_col: Engineering Teams receive notification that the spec is approved and available for CLI emission.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall notify Engineering Teams subscribed to the project that the spec has transitioned to 'approved' status.

ending_conditions: The spec's status is exactly one of: approved, rejected, revisions_requested.
An audit entry records the state transition with reviewer, prior state, new state, comment count, and timestamp.
The Founder has been notified of the review decision and any attached comments.
On approve, the CLI-emission pipeline is enabled and Engineering Teams subscribed to the project are notified.
On reject or revisions_requested, the CLI-emission pipeline remains blocked.
notes: Scope boundary: UC03 covers the review decision only. Founder resubmission after 'revisions_requested' loops back through UC01 (re-draft).
State machine: draft → {approved, rejected, revisions_requested}. revisions_requested → draft on Founder resubmission. rejected is terminal unless the Founder clones the spec.
Cross-cutting: session auth (CC.R01), audit trail (CC.R02), RBAC on review permission (CC.R03).
Request-revisions action requires at least one review comment (step 13) to avoid vague-feedback anti-pattern.
AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS bounds how long review audit entries are retained; set in constants table per compliance refs.
Secondary actor Founder receives notification only; they do not act in this UCBD. Separate flow if the Founder disputes the review decision.
Engineering Teams notification on approve lets the project organization pull the CLI bundle without a separate trigger.

extends_from_elsewhere: UC04 extends UC03 when state transitions to approved
